questions.question: The Nuakhai festival is predominantly observed in which state? > नुआखाई त्योहार मुख्य रूप से किस राज्य में मनाया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Nuakhai or Navakhai or is an agricultural festival mainly observed by people of Western Odisha and Southern Chhattisgarh in India. Punjab is known for its vibrant festivals, with Baisakhi [April 14] and Lohri [January 13] being the most prominent. Major festivals of Uttar Pradesh include Diwali, Holi, Dussehra and Krishna Janmashtami. > नुआखाई या नवाखाई एक कृषि त्यौहार है जो मुख्य रूप से भारत में पश्चिमी ओडिशा और दक्षिणी छत्तीसगढ़ के लोगों द्वारा मनाया जाता है। पंजाब अपने जीवंत त्योहारों के लिए जाना जाता है, जिनमें बैसाखी [14 अप्रैल] और लोहड़ी [13 जनवरी] सबसे प्रमुख हैं। उत्तर प्रदेश के प्रमुख त्योहारों में दिवाली, होली, दशहरा और कृष्ण जन्माष्टमी शामिल हैं।

questions.question: Who among the following was the first Viceroy of India?
questions.explanation: Lord Canning was the first Viceroy of India. His tenure lasted for four years between 1858 and 1862. Canning introduced several reforms, including abolishing the Doctrine of Lapse, restructuring the army and finances, and establishing universities and high courts. Lord Dalhousie was the Governor-General of India from 1848 to 1856. He was famous for his Doctrine of Lapse policy. Lord William Bentinck was a British governor-general of Bengal. Lord Lytton was an English government official who served as Viceroy of India from 1876 to 1880.

questions.question: Guru Ghasidas tiger reserve” is located in which of the following Indian state?
questions.explanation: Guru Ghasidas Tiger Reserve is located in Koriya and Surajpur district of Chhattisgarh state of India. It is the 56th Tiger Reserve of India to be declared in 2024. Guru Ghasidas-Tamor Tiger Reserve got final approval for notification from the National Tiger Conservation Authority in October 2021. Rajasthan has four officially designated tiger reserves: Ranthambore, Sariska, Mukundara Hills, and Ramgarh Vishdhari. Palamau Tiger Reserve is a tiger reserve in India and the only one in the state of Jharkhand.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor had Navaratna’s in his court?
questions.explanation: The Navratnas of Akbar [also called Nine Gems] were nine distinguished individuals in Emperor Akbar's court, celebrated for their brilliance in various fields such as administration, literature, music, and military strategy. Abul Fazl, Abdul Rahim Khan-i-Khana, Birbal, Faizi, Todar Mal, Raja Man Singh, Mulla Do Piaza, Tansen, and Fakir Aziao-Din. Babur is famous for founding the Mughal Empire in India and winning pivotal battles like the First Battle of Panipat in 1526.

questions.question: World Wetland day is celebrated every year on__.
questions.explanation: World Wetlands Day is celebrated every year on February 2nd. This day marks the anniversary of the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands [Ramsar Convention] on February 2, 1971, in Ramsar, Iran. World Cancer Day is celebrated every year on February 4th. Some other important days- 23 Jan- Parakram Diwas 25 Dec- Cristmas Day 9 Jan- Pravasi Bharatiya Divas 12 Jan- National Youth Day.

questions.question: Which state had the first female Governor of independent India?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was the first woman to become the governor of an Indian state. She governed Uttar Pradesh from 15 August 1947 to 2 March 1949. Govind Ballabh Pant was the first chief minister of Uttar Pradesh who served from 1950 to 1954. Rajendra Prasad served as the first President of India, holding the office from January 26, 1950, until May 13, 1962.

questions.question: Which of the following river is known as Dakshina Ganga?
questions.explanation: River Godavari is also known as Dakshin Ganga. It is the largest peninsular river system. It rises in the Nasik district of Maharashtra. The Ganga River is a cultural icon in India, flowing from the Himalayas to the Bay of Bengal, It's the longest river in India, with a length of 2,525 km. The Mahanadi River is a major river in East Central India. The Krishna River originates in the Western Ghats near Mahabaleshwar, Maharashtra.

questions.question: How many days did Gandhi take to complete the Dandi March?
questions.explanation: The Dandi March, also known as the Salt March, was a crucial event in India's independence struggle. It took Mahatma Gandhi and his followers 24 days to complete the Dandi March. The march began from the Sabarmati Ashram on 12 March 1930 and ended in the coastal town of Dandi on 6 April 1930. Some other important movements of Gandhi Ji- Champaran Satyagraha (1917), Kheda Satyagraha (1918), Non-cooperation Movement (1920), Quit India Movement (1942).

questions.question: What is the name of largest moon in the solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter's moon Ganymede is the largest satellite in the solar system. The satellite is even larger than Mercury and Pluto, and only slightly smaller than Mars. Phobos and Deimos are the moons of Mars. They are among the smallest moons in our solar system. Europa is Jupiter's fourth-largest moon and the sixth-largest in the Solar System.

questions.question: What is the rank of India in the Global Terrorism Index 2025?
questions.explanation: India ranks 14th in the Global Terrorism Index [GTI] 2025. The headquarters of the Global Terrorism Index [GTI] is located in Sydney, Australia. Islamic State [IS] has been described as the world's most dangerous terrorist organization. According this report the number of countries affected by terrorism has increased.

questions.question: Which country is home to Mount Kilimanjaro?
questions.explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro, the highest peak in Africa, is located in Tanzania, a country in East Africa. It's a large, free standing volcano, with three prominent cones: Kibo, Mawenzi, and Shira. The highest peak in the United States is Denali [formerly Mount McKinley], located in Alaska. Mount Kosciuszko is the highest peak in mainland Australia, standing at 2,228 meters. The highest peak in Japan is Mount Fuji.

questions.question: Recently, where was the meeting of Defense Ministers of Shanghai Cooperation Organization [SCO] 2024 held?
questions.explanation: Defence Secretary Shri Giridhar Aramane was leading the Indian delegation to Astana, Kazakhstan from April 25-26, 2024 for the Annual Meeting of Defence Ministers of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation [SCO] Member States.

questions.question: Who was the last emperor of the Mauryan Dynasty?
questions.explanation: The last emperor of the Mauryan Dynasty was Brihadratha Maurya. He was assassinated by his general, Pushyamitra Sunga, who then founded the Shunga dynasty. Kanishka was the greatest king of the Kushan dynasty. Kanishka's main capital was Purushapura [modern-day Peshawar]. Chandragupta Maurya was the founder and the first ruler of the Maurya Dynasty. Bindusara [320 BCE – 273 BCE] was the second Mauryan emperor of Magadha.

questions.question: Which Constitutional amendment provided for Goods and Services Tax [GST] in India?
questions.explanation: The Constitution [101ˢᵗ] Amendment Act, 2016 allows both the centre and states to levy the Goods and Services Tax [GST]. The 102nd Constitutional Amendment Act of 2018 granted constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes [NCBC]. The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 2019 reserves 10% of seats in educational institutions and government jobs for Economically Weaker Sections [EWS]. The 104th Constitutional Amendment Act of 2020 extends SCs/STs seat reservations in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies until 2030.

questions.question: Who launched the one district one product scheme?
questions.explanation: ODOP was launched by the Ministry of Food Processing Industries, to help districts reach their full potential, Identify, brand, and promote a single, iconic product from each district, showcasing its unique strengths, foster economic and socio-cultural growth, and create employment opportunities, especially, in rural areas.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest gland in human body?
questions.explanation: The liver is the largest gland in the human body. It's located in the upper right portion of the abdominal cavity and plays a crucial role in digestion and various metabolic processes. The kidneys filter waste materials from the blood and regulate water, salt, and mineral balance. Two lungs make oxygen available to our body and remove gases like carbon dioxide. The thyroid gland is a vital endocrine gland responsible for producing hormones that regulate metabolism, growth, and development.

questions.question: From which Constitution did the makers of the Indian Constitution borrow the idea of ​​single citizenship?
questions.explanation: India has a system of single citizenship, meaning all citizens are citizens of India, regardless of their state or regional origin. The idea of single citizenship in the Indian Constitution was borrowed from the British Constitution. The Canadian Constitution was adopted, including the idea of a federal system and the protection of human rights. The Concurrent List, freedom of trade and commerce from the Australian Constitution. The Indian Constitution adopted the concept of Fundamental Duties from the Russian Constitution.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Voice –Chairperson of the Uttar Pradesh Woman Commission in 2024?
questions.explanation: The Uttar Pradesh government has appointed Aparna Yadav as vice-chairperson of the women's commission, Babita Chauhan as the new chairperson.

questions.question: What is the process by which a gas is converted into a solid?
questions.explanation: The direct transformation of a gas into a solid without becoming a liquid is called deposition, also known as desublimation. Evaporation is the process where a liquid, like water, transforms into a gas, also known as vapor. Freezing is a phase transition in which a liquid turns into a solid when its temperature is lowered below its freezing point. Filtration is a process that involves the separation of a solid substance from a liquid.

questions.question: Which of the following areas make the largest contribution to national income in India?
questions.explanation: The Service Sector makes the largest contribution to India's national income. The service sector accounts for the majority of India's Gross Domestic Product [GDP], often exceeding 50%. The service sector is also known for its rapid growth and its ability to create employment opportunities. The agriculture sector contributes about 18.2% to India's gross domestic product [GDP], the industry sector contributes 29%.

questions.question: Which of the following disease is transmitted by Tse-Tse flies?
questions.explanation: The disease transmitted by Tse-tse flies is sleeping sickness. Sleeping sickness, also known as African trypanosomiasis, is caused by the parasite Trypanosoma brucei. Dengue fever is caused by a viral infection that is spread by the bite of infected Aedes aegypti mosquitoes. Kala-azar, also known as visceral leishmaniasis, is caused by a protozoan parasite, Leishmania donovani. Malaria is a disease caused by a parasite.

questions.question: Match the following:- List-1 List-2 A) Terhatali 1) Uttar Pradesh B) Kathak 2) Goa C) Fugdi 3) Rajasthan D) Bihu 4) Assam
questions.explanation: Terhatali is a famous folk dance of Rajasthan, Kathak is a classical Indian dance form that originated in Uttar Pradesh, Fugdi is a traditional folk dance of Goa and The Bihu dance is a folk dance originating from the Indian state of Assam.

questions.question: Which gass was used in First World War?
questions.explanation: During the First World War, several types of gases were used as chemical weapons, including chlorine, phosgene, and mustard gas. Nitrous oxide, also known as laughing gas, is a colorless, non-flammable gas. Ammonium chloride [NH₄Cl] is a white crystalline salt. Methane [CH4] is a crucial natural gas.

questions.question: What does D stand for in NDTL used in economics?
questions.explanation: Net Demand and Time Liabilities [NDTL] is a term used in banking to calculate the total liabilities of a bank. It includes the sum of demand liabilities [like current deposits and savings accounts] and time liabilities [like fixed deposits], minus the assets held by the bank within the banking system. NDTL is crucial for determining regulatory requirements such as the Cash Reserve Ratio [CRR] and Statutory Liquidity Ratio [SLR], which banks must maintain as per guidelines set by the Reserve Bank of India. In the economics term NDTL, D stands for Demand. This term is crucial in understanding a bank's liquidity and its obligations to depositors.

questions.question: Car moving on a straight road is an example of _________ motion.
questions.explanation: A car moving on a straight road is an example of rectilinear motion. This is because the car's path is a straight line. This type of motion perfectly describes the car's movement along the straight road.

questions.explanation: As P can beat Q by 200 m or by 20 seconds; i.e. Q can cover 200m in 20 seconds, therefore Speed of Q = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699705c425e960.25587167.gif) = 10 m/s Time taken by Q to cover 1200m = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699705c4461c61.39867473.gif) = 120 seconds And thus, time taken by P to cover 1200m = 120 – 20 = 100 seconds Hence, speed of P = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699705c45fd956.66097931.gif) = 12m/s > चूँकि P, Q को 200 मीटर या 20 सेकंड से हरा सकता है; अर्थात Q, 20 सेकंड में 200 मीटर की दूरी तय कर सकता है, इसलिए Q की गति = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699705c4770e52.26047162.gif) = 10 मीटर/सेकंड Q द्वारा 1200 मीटर की दूरी तय करने में लिया गया समय = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699705c48586f3.58364415.gif) = 120 सेकंड और इस प्रकार, P द्वारा 1200 मीटर की दूरी तय करने में लिया गया समय = 120 - 20 = 100 सेकंड इसलिए, P की गति = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699705c4959300.66216904.gif) = 12 मीटर/सेकंड

questions.question: The numbers of students who passed with different honours from a college during 2015 to 2018 are depicted in the given table: Year Mathematics Social Studies English Hindi Economics 2015 37 26 18 16 38 2016 32 16 21 23 30 2017 41 15 29 33 30 2018 29 09 31 32 42 Find the ratio of students passed with Social Studies & Economics to those with Mathematics & English.
questions.explanation: Number of students passed with Social Studies = 26+16+15+09 = 66 Number of students passed with Economics = 38+30+30+42 = 140 Number of students passed with Mathematics = 37+32+41+29 = 139 Number of students passed with English = 18+21+29+31 = 99 Hence, ratio of students passed with Social Studies & Economics to those with Mathematics & English = [66+140] : [139+99] = 206:238 = 103:119

questions.question: Effective time management is the backbone of productivity and success in both personal and professional life. In today's fast-paced world, where demands on our time are endless, mastering the art of managing time is more ____1____ than ever. Time management is not just about getting more done in less time; it's about making the most of the time we have to achieve our goals, reduce stress, and improve our overall quality of life. The foundation of good time management ____2____ in setting clear goals and priorities. Without a clear understanding of what needs to be accomplished, it's easy to get bogged ____3____ in trivial tasks that consume time without contributing significantly to our objectives. Prioritizing tasks based on their urgency and importance ____4____ that we're allocating our time wisely, tackling high-priority tasks first. Effective time management also involves learning to say no. Many people struggle with over-committing themselves, taking on too many tasks or responsibilities because they find it difficult to ____5____ requests from others. Learning to say no without feeling guilty or anxious is essential for protecting our time and energy for the tasks that truly matter. It's about being mindful of our limits and prioritizing our own needs and goals. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The word "crucial" means "extremely important or vital" which fits perfectly in this context, implying that mastering time management is of utmost importance in today's fast-paced world. The other options don't fit as well: - Futile: Means "having no purpose or effect" which is the opposite of what the sentence is trying to convey. - Trivial: Means "unimportant or insignificant" which doesn't fit the context. - Imperial: Refers to something related to an empire or imperial system, which doesn't fit the context.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment: serve dinner while the Explanation: • The verb "serve" is incorrect here because it does not match the passive voice structure that is needed. • This sentence describes an action happening to the guests [they are receiving dinner], so it should be in the passive voice. • The sentence describes an action being done to the guests, so it should be in the past continuous passive form. • The correct verb form is "being served", which is:   [were + being + past participle] Corrected Sentence: The guests were being served dinner while the host was speaking.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. His joke really broke the ________ at the party.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Ice Explanation: The phrase "broke the ice" is a well-known idiom that means to make people feel more comfortable or to relieve tension in a social situation. It fits perfectly in this context where the joke is making the party atmosphere more relaxed. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Monotony – उबाऊपन, नीरसता – Refers to something repetitive or dull. Example: The monotony of the office routine made her feel exhausted. • Silence – चुप्प – Refers to the absence of sound, but it is not used in the idiom. Example: The silence in the room was uncomfortable. • Ice – बर्फ – This is the correct word in the context of the idiom "break the ice," meaning to ease tension. Example: His joke really broke the ice at the party. • Atmosphere – माहौल, वातावरण – Refers to the overall feeling or mood, but it’s not the idiom used here. Example: The atmosphere at the concert was electrifying. Final Correct Sentence: "His joke really broke the ice at the party."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The mob beaten the thief, didn’t they?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Didn’t it? Explanation: • The sentence is in the past simple tense: "The mob beaten the thief..." • However, "beaten" is incorrect here. The past simple form of "beat" is "beat", not "beaten" [which is the past participle used in perfect tenses]. • Also, the question tag must match the subject and the tense of the main sentence. • The subject "mob" is a collective noun and is treated as singular, so the correct pronoun is "it". • The tense is past simple, so the auxiliary "did" is used in the question tag. Corrected Sentence: The mob beat the thief, didn’t it?

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: She will have finished the work by the time her boss will arrive.
She would have finished the work by the time her boss arrives.
She will have finished the work by the time her boss arrives.
She will have finished the work by the time her boss is arriving.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: She will have finished the work by the time her boss arrives. Explanation: • This sentence is grammatically correct and follows the rule for combining Future Perfect Tense with a Present Indefinite clause introduced by "by the time." • "She will have finished" is in future perfect tense, showing that the action will be completed before a certain point in the future. • "Her boss arrives" is in present indefinite tense, which is the correct structure after time conjunctions like "by the time." Concept Applied: • Structure: Future Perfect + by the time + Present Indefinite • Usage: To describe an action that will be completed before another future event Final Correct Sentence: She will have finished the work by the time her boss arrives.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. We will call out your office around 3 PM to drop off the documents.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Call at Explanation: • The phrasal verb "call at" is used when referring to visiting a place, especially a short or formal visit, which fits this sentence describing someone going to an office to drop off documents. • The phrasal verb "call out" means to shout or to challenge someone, which is incorrect in this context. Meanings and examples of the options: • Call at – किसी स्थान पर जाना/रुकना – To visit or stop at a place Example: The ship will call at Mumbai before heading to Dubai. • Call after – के नाम पर नाम रखना – To name someone after another Example: The baby was called after his grandfather. • Call upon – औपचारिक अनुरोध करना – To formally request or ask someone to do something Example: I now call upon the speaker to present her views. • Call out – जोर से पुकारना / आलोचना करना – To shout or openly criticise Example: He called out her mistake in front of everyone. Final Correct Sentence: We will call at your office around 3 PM to drop off the documents.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. They deal _____ antique furniture and rare collectibles.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: In Explanation: • The verb "deal in" is commonly used when referring to a business or trade activity involving a particular type of goods or service. • "Deal in" refers to being involved in buying and selling a particular product or type of item. Examples: • They deal in antique furniture and rare collectibles. • This shop deals in vintage books. "Deal with" is used when talking about handling or managing something, but for commerce or trading, "deal in" is the correct phrase. Corrected Sentence: "They deal in antique furniture and rare collectibles."

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: It was him Explanation: • If a sentence starts with 'it' followed by any form of 'be' the pronoun that follows 'be' must be in 'nominative case.’ • The pronoun "him" is in the objective case, but after the verb "was," we need the nominative case, which is "he." • The structure "It was he" is grammatically correct when used with a linking verb like "was." • "He" is the subject of the clause "who finished the project early," so it must be in the nominative case. Examples: • It was he who finished the project early. ✔ • It was him who finished the project early. ❌ Corrected Sentence: "It was he who finished the project early."

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: A. if we take the time to truly observe and embrace B. the natural wonders that surround us. C. It’s a reminder that, even in a busy and chaotic world D. moments of pure serenity can be found
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: CDAB Explanation: Let’s break it down step by step: • C introduces the idea that, even in a busy and chaotic world, nature offers a reminder. • D continues by explaining that moments of serenity can be found despite the chaos. • A adds that these moments are accessible if we take the time to truly observe. • B concludes by mentioning the natural wonders that surround us, tying everything together. Final Paragraph: It’s a reminder that, even in a busy and chaotic world, moments of pure serenity can be found if we take the time to truly observe and embrace the natural wonders that surround us.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The milk smelt fresh.
options: The milk is fresh when smelt.
The milk was fresh when it was smelt.
The milk was fresh when it was smelling.
The milk is fresh when it was smelt.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: The milk was fresh when it was smelt. Explanation: • The original sentence "The milk smelt fresh" is in active voice, with the subject "the milk" and the verb "smelt" [past tense of "smell" used in a sensory context]. • To change this to passive voice, we convert it to: was + past participle [smelt] Final Sentence: The milk was fresh when it was smelt.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. He said, "Will she come to the party or not?"
options: He asked whether she would come to the party or not.
He asked that would she come to the party or not.
He said whether she will come to the party or not.
He asked if she will come to the party or not.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The reporting verb "asked" is used because it's a question. • In indirect speech, the question format changes to a statement format — no question word order. • "Will" becomes "would" due to the shift in tense. • "Whether" is correctly used in indirect speech to report a question with alternatives [come or not]. Final Sentence: He asked whether she would come to the party or not.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The abandoned house had a stunning appearance, with broken windows and overgrown vines covering its walls.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Hideous Stunning - आश्चर्यजनक - Extremely impressive or beautiful; causing astonishment. Attractive - आकर्षक - Pleasing or appealing to the senses or mind. Slight - थोड़ा - Small in degree, amount, or extent; not much. Hideous - घृणित - Extremely ugly or repulsive; unpleasant to look at. Non-monotonous - एकरसता से मुक्त - Not dull or repetitive; varied or lively in nature.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. MUMBO JUMBO
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Nonsense Let’s see the meaning of all words, Mumbo jumbo - बकवास - Refers to confusing or meaningless language or activity, often used to describe something that appears complicated but is ultimately unnecessary or confusing.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. DISCERN
questions.explanation: The correct synonym is – Perceive Let’s see the meaning of all words, Discern - पहचानना - To recognize or identify something clearly, especially after careful thought. Cupidity - लोभ - Greed, especially for wealth or material gain. Debilitate - कमजोर करना - To weaken or impair the strength or vitality of something or someone. Strange - अजीब - Unusual, unfamiliar, or difficult to understand. Perceive - महसूस करना - To become aware of or understand something through the senses or the mind.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The students were required to conform to the school's dress code by wearing uniforms.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Deviation Let’s see the meaning of all words, Conform - अनुरूप होना - To comply with rules, standards, or laws; to behave according to socially accepted conventions. Acquiesce - स्वीकार करना - To accept or agree to something reluctantly but without protest. Deviation - विचलन - A departure from an established course, standard, or norm. Expansion - विस्तार - The process of increasing in size, number, or importance. Indifferent - उदासीन - Showing no interest or concern; having no particular preference or opinion.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A very small village
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Hamlet Let’s see the meaning of all words, Hanger - कपड़ा लटकाने का सहारा - A device used to hang clothes, typically a curved piece of wood, metal, or plastic. Hangar - हैंगर - A large building or structure used for storing aircraft. Gust - झोंका - A strong, brief burst of wind. Hamlet - ग्राम - A small village or settlement, usually smaller than a town.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. A shady place under tree
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Bower Let’s see the meaning of all words, Bower - आश्रय - A pleasant, shady place under trees or climbing plants, often a resting place. Brigand - लुटेरा - A member of a gang of robbers or bandits, especially in the past. Fallow - बंजर - Land left unplanted or unused for a period of time to restore its fertility. Huffy - चिढ़ा हुआ - Easily irritated or offended; quick to show displeasure.
